examples.title: Collections before and after generics
examples.description: Side-by-side comparison of `ArrayList` with its required casts and boxing against the type-safe, zero-overhead `List<int>` equivalent.

examples.title: A single generic method works across all types
examples.description: One `Filter<T>` method replaces writing a separate version for every element type, with full compile-time type safety.

examples.title: Type constraints unlock meaningful generic behavior
examples.description: A `where T : IComparable<T>` constraint lets the compiler verify that `T` supports comparison, enabling a generic `Max` and `Clamp` that work for any comparable type.

relatedFeatures.reason: Continue to LINQ to see generics at the center of the most widely used query API in .NET.
Explore Lambda expressions to see how generic delegates like `Func<T>` and `Action<T>` combine with anonymous functions.
Explore Extension methods to understand how LINQ's `IEnumerable<T>` API is built on top of generic types.
